=== Quick Fix Inventory ===

[D1] (9:14-9:30) TS2322: Type 'Effect<number, ErrorA | ErrorB, never>' is not assignable to type 'Effect<number, never, never>'.
  Type 'ErrorA | ErrorB' is not assignable to type 'never'.
    Type 'ErrorA' is not assignable to type 'never'.
  (no quick fixes)

[D2] (9:14-9:30) TS377003: Missing errors ErrorA | ErrorB in the expected Effect type. effect(missingEffectError)
  Fix 0: "Disable missingEffectError for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable missingEffectError for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Catch all errors with Effect.catch"
  Fix 3: "Catch unexpected errors with Effect.catchTag"

[D3] (12:14-12:27) TS2322: Type 'Effect<number, ErrorA | ErrorB, never>' is not assignable to type 'Effect<number, ErrorA, never>'.
  Type 'ErrorA | ErrorB' is not assignable to type 'ErrorA'.
    Type 'ErrorB' is not assignable to type 'ErrorA'.
      Types of property '_tag' are incompatible.
        Type '"ErrorB"' is not assignable to type '"ErrorA"'.
  (no quick fixes)

[D4] (12:14-12:27) TS377003: Missing errors ErrorB in the expected Effect type. effect(missingEffectError)
  Fix 0: "Disable missingEffectError for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable missingEffectError for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Catch unexpected errors with Effect.catchTag"

=== Quick Fix Application Results ===

=== [D2] Fix 0: "Disable missingEffectError for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D2] Fix 1: "Disable missingEffectError for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D2] Fix 2: "Catch all errors with Effect.catch" ===

--- file:///.src/missingEffectError.ts ---
import { Effect, Data } from "effect"

class ErrorA extends Data.TaggedError("ErrorA")<{ message: string }> {}
class ErrorB extends Data.TaggedError("ErrorB")<{ code: number }> {}

declare const effectWithErrors: Effect.Effect<number, ErrorA | ErrorB>

// Should trigger missingEffectError - assigning Effect with errors to Effect<number> (no errors)
export const missingAllErrors: Effect.Effect<number> = Effect.catch(effectWithErrors, () => Effect.dieMessage("TODO: catch not implemented"))

// Should trigger missingEffectError - missing ErrorB
export const missingErrorB: Effect.Effect<number, ErrorA> = effectWithErrors

// Should NOT trigger - errors match
export const errorsMatch: Effect.Effect<number, ErrorA | ErrorB> = effectWithErrors

// Should NOT trigger - no errors in source
declare const effectNoErrors: Effect.Effect<number>
export const noSourceErrors: Effect.Effect<number> = effectNoErrors


=== [D2] Fix 3: "Catch unexpected errors with Effect.catchTag" ===

--- file:///.src/missingEffectError.ts ---
import { Effect, Data } from "effect"

class ErrorA extends Data.TaggedError("ErrorA")<{ message: string }> {}
class ErrorB extends Data.TaggedError("ErrorB")<{ code: number }> {}

declare const effectWithErrors: Effect.Effect<number, ErrorA | ErrorB>

// Should trigger missingEffectError - assigning Effect with errors to Effect<number> (no errors)
export const missingAllErrors: Effect.Effect<number> = Effect.catchTags(effectWithErrors, { "ErrorA": () => Effect.dieMessage("TODO: catchTags() not implemented for ErrorA"), "ErrorB": () => Effect.dieMessage("TODO: catchTags() not implemented for ErrorB") })

// Should trigger missingEffectError - missing ErrorB
export const missingErrorB: Effect.Effect<number, ErrorA> = effectWithErrors

// Should NOT trigger - errors match
export const errorsMatch: Effect.Effect<number, ErrorA | ErrorB> = effectWithErrors

// Should NOT trigger - no errors in source
declare const effectNoErrors: Effect.Effect<number>
export const noSourceErrors: Effect.Effect<number> = effectNoErrors


=== [D4] Fix 0: "Disable missingEffectError for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D4] Fix 1: "Disable missingEffectError for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D4] Fix 2: "Catch unexpected errors with Effect.catchTag" ===

--- file:///.src/missingEffectError.ts ---
import { Effect, Data } from "effect"

class ErrorA extends Data.TaggedError("ErrorA")<{ message: string }> {}
class ErrorB extends Data.TaggedError("ErrorB")<{ code: number }> {}

declare const effectWithErrors: Effect.Effect<number, ErrorA | ErrorB>

// Should trigger missingEffectError - assigning Effect with errors to Effect<number> (no errors)
export const missingAllErrors: Effect.Effect<number> = effectWithErrors

// Should trigger missingEffectError - missing ErrorB
export const missingErrorB: Effect.Effect<number, ErrorA> = Effect.catchTags(effectWithErrors, { "ErrorB": () => Effect.dieMessage("TODO: catchTags() not implemented for ErrorB") })

// Should NOT trigger - errors match
export const errorsMatch: Effect.Effect<number, ErrorA | ErrorB> = effectWithErrors

// Should NOT trigger - no errors in source
declare const effectNoErrors: Effect.Effect<number>
export const noSourceErrors: Effect.Effect<number> = effectNoErrors
